title: sclerostemma rupestre
intro: Sclerostemma rupestre, also known as the tree yam or climbing yam, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sclerostrema rupestre include its ability to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Sclerostemma rupestre can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various ailments related to the skin and digestive system.
